evidence: Zo feels "too complicated" and lacks mental space for building from scratch. They discussed the "blank slate" trap common in AI products.
V has refined a specialized layer on top of vanilla Zo and exported a version to share.
V is demoing for the Zo team (specifically founder Ben) on Wednesday. Noted a shift in how the Zo founders view him—from an "enthusiastic user" to someone with "valuable go-to-market insights."
V highlighted a significant breakthrough: the ability for Zo to switch personas agentically (e.g., Builder to Researcher to Writer) based on the task, using Sonnet 4.5.
Nafisa noted the lack of a native voice-to-text feature in the Zo interface, which V agreed would be a valuable addition for mobile use.
V is leaning towards giving it [persona setup] all away as a show of faith.
V discussed the need for a standardized "ingestion function" to help users absorb his architectural principles into their own Zo systems.
Nafisa identified meeting notes generation as the most universally desired workflow.
One doctor emphasized strict diet and lifestyle changes to manage stress (cortisol-sugar correlation).
